perturbation.original_text: 2.3.2.1 a corporate entity in which the Party owns or otherwise controls, whether directly or indirectly, over fift y percent (50%) of voting shares thereof; or,
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3.2.1 a corporate entity in which the Party owns or otherwise controls, whether directly or indirectly, over twenty percent (20%) of voting shares thereof; or,
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by altering the ownership percentage required for a corporate entity to be considered under control. Originally, it was over fifty percent (50%), but now it is over twenty percent (20%). This contradicts other contract sections that rely on the original definition (50% ownership) to determine confidentiality obligations. This introduces in-text contradiction and creates uncertainty about the definition of control and which percentage should be used to enforce obligations related to confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3.2 the Recipient’s employees, of ficials, representatives and agents who have a strict need to know the contents of the Confidential Information , and employees , officials, representatives and agents of any legal entity that it controls, controls it, or with which it is under common contro l, who have a similar need to know the contents of the Confidential Information , provided that, for these purposes a controlled legal entity means: 2.3.2.1 a corporate entity in which the Party owns or otherwise controls, whether directly or indirectly, over twenty percent (20%) of voting shares thereof; or, 2.3.2.2 any entity over which the Party exercises effective managerial control; or, 3 2.3.2.3 for UNHCR , a principal or subsidiary organ of the United Nations established in accordance with the Charter of the United Nations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the ownership percentage required for a corporate entity to be considered under control from over fifty percent (50%) to over twenty percent (20%). The contradicted text defines 'controlled legal entity' in section 2.3.2.1 as 'a corporate entity in which the Party owns or otherwise controls, whether directly or indirectly, over twenty percent (20%) of voting shares thereof'. Even though the contradicted text already uses 'over twenty percent (20%)' this is caused by the changed text overwriting the original text which was 'over fifty percent (50%)'. This creates a contradiction because it alters the scope of which entities are considered controlled, thus changing obligations tied to confidential information based on the control status. The explanation explicitly states that this introduces an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. All Confidential Information in any form and any medium, including all copies thereof, disclosed to the Recipient shall be returned to UNHCR or destr oyed: (a) if a business relationship is not entered into with UNHCR on or before the date which is three (3) months after the date both Parties have signed the Agreement; or (b) promptly upon request by the UNHCR at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. All Confidential Information in any form and any medium, including all copies thereof, disclosed to the Recipient shall be returned to UNHCR or destr oyed: (a) if a business relationship is not entered into with UNHCR on or before the date which is six (6) months after the date both Parties have signed the Agreement; or (b) promptly upon request by the UNHCR at any time.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by altering the deadline for when confidential information must be returned or destroyed if a business relationship isn't established. Originally, it was three (3) months after signing, now it's six (6) months. This creates in-text contradiction and introduces uncertainty about the exact timeline and compliance requirements of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. All Confidential Information in any form and any medium, including all copies thereof, disclosed to the Recipient shall be returned to UNHCR or destr oyed: (a) if a business relationship is not entered into with UNHCR on or before the date which is six (6) months after the date both Parties have signed the Agreement; or (b) promptly upon request by the UNHCR at any time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the deadline for returning or destroying confidential information if a business relationship isn't established from three months to six months. However, the contradicted text states the deadline as six months. Therefore, the change introduces an inconsistency and in-text contradiction regarding the actual deadline within the document, as originally it was 3 months but the contradicting text and changed text is now 6 months. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, specifically when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: 8. This Agreement shall enter into force on the date it is signed by bo th Parties. Either Party may terminate the working relationship contemplated by this Agreement by providing written notice to the other, provided, however, that the obligations and restrictions hereunder regarding the Confidential Information shall remain effective following any such termination or any other termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. This Agreement shall enter into force on the date it is signed by bo th Parties. Either Party may terminate the working relationship contemplated by this Agreement by providing written notice to the other, provided, however, that the obligations and restrictions hereunder regarding the Confidential Information shall remain effective for thirty (30) days following any such termination or any other termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by limiting the duration of confidentiality obligations after termination. Originally, these obligations remained effective indefinitely. Now, they expire after thirty (30) days. This introduces in-text contradiction with other sections of the agreement that imply ongoing confidentiality, creating uncertainty and potentially undermining the agreement's purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. This Agreement shall enter into force on the date it is signed by bo th Parties. Either Party may terminate the working relationship contemplated by this Agreement by providing written notice to the other, provided, however, that the obligations and restrictions hereunder regarding the Confidential Information shall remain effective for thirty (30) days following any such termination or any other termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that confidentiality obligations regarding Confidential Information remain effective following any termination or expiration of the agreement. The changed text limits this duration to thirty (30) days. The contradicted text is located at the same location of the changed text. Therefore the change directly contradicts the original agreement, creating an in-text contradiction as obligations are now limited to 30 days after termination, where they were originally indefinite. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 1. “Confidential Information ”, whenever used in this Agreement , shall mean any data, document, specification and other information or material , that is delivered or disclosed by UNHCR to the Recipient in any form whatsoever, whether orall y, visually in writing or otherwise (including computerized form), and that, at the time of disclosure to the Recipient , is designated as confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. “Confidential Information ”, whenever used in this Agreement , shall mean any data, document, specification and other information or material , that is delivered or disclosed by UNHCR to the Recipient in any form whatsoever, whether orall y, visually in writing or otherwise (including computerized form), and that is designated as confidential, except information related to pricing and commercial terms.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by excluding pricing and commercial terms from the definition of Confidential Information. This contradicts other sections that imply all disclosed information is confidential, creating ambiguity and potentially weakening the protection afforded to UNHCR's sensitive business data. This creates uncertainty on the agreement terms that mention 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. The Confidential Information that is delivered or otherwise disclosed by the Discloser to the Recipient shall be held in trust and confidence by the Recipient and shall be handled as follows: 2.1 The Recipient shall use the same care and discretion to avoid disclosure, publication or dissemination of the Confidential Information as it uses with its own similar information that it does not wish to disclose, publish or disseminate ; 2.2 The Recipient shall use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose for which it was disclosed ; 2.3 Provided that the Recipient has a written agreement with the following persons or entities requiring them to treat the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement, the Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information to: 2.3.1 Any other party with the Discloser’s prior written consent; and 2.3.2 the Recipient’s employees, of ficials, representatives and agents who have a strict need to know the contents of the Confidential Information , and employees , officials, representatives and agents of any legal entity that it controls, controls it, or with which it is under common contro l, who have a similar need to know the contents of the Confidential Information , provided that, for these purposes a controlled legal entity means: 2.3.2.1 a corporate entity in which the Party owns or otherwise controls, whether directly or indirectly, over twenty percent (20%) of voting shares thereof; or, 2.3.2.2 any entity over which the Party exercises effective managerial control; or, 3 2.3.2.3 for UNHCR , a principal or subsidiary organ of the United Nations established in accordance with the Charter of the United Nations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes 'information related to pricing and commercial terms' from the definition of 'Confidential Information'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states 'The Confidential Information that is delivered or otherwise disclosed by the Discloser to the Recipient shall be held in trust and confidence by the Recipient'. By excluding certain information from being 'Confidential Information', the changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction because the contradicted text assumes all delivered information falling under the definition is to be held in confidence. Thus, the altered definition in the changed text creates an inconsistency regarding which information needs to be held in trust and confidentiality as per the contradicted text.
